notes: The source_filing field claims this is a Johnson & Johnson 10-K (FY2025) under CIK 19617, but CIK 19617 is JPMORGAN CHASE & CO — not Johnson & Johnson. The source_url https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/19617/000162828026008131/jpm-20251231.htm resolves to JPMorgan's FY2025 10-K (the 'jpm-' filename prefix and the accession filed 2026-02-13 under CIK 19617 both confirm this). The test case even internally acknowledges the contradiction by describing the accession as 'JPMorgan-labeled' while attributing it to J&J. Johnson & Johnson's actual FY2025 10-K is filed under CIK 0000200406, accession 0000200406-26-000016, primary document jnj-20251228.htm. The original_text, while boilerplate that does appear in genuine J&J 10-Ks (confirmed via EDGAR full-text search hits for CIK 0000200406), cannot be verified as coming from the cited JPMorgan document at the given URL. The strip-caveat hallucination construct, citation label, verdict mapping, and subtlety rating are all internally sound; the fatal flaw is the source metadata pointing to the wrong company and wrong filing entirely."
suggested_correction: Replace source_filing, source_url, and CIK to point to the actual Johnson & Johnson FY2025 10-K: CIK 0000200406, accession 0000200406-26-000016, URL https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/200406/000020040626000016/jnj-20251228.htm. Then verify that the original_text verbatim appears in jnj-20251228.htm under the Regulation/Environmental section before re-promoting this case to the corpus.
haiku_reason: The claim uses standard SEC filing boilerplate language with appropriate legal qualification ("in all material respects"). It appears in the expected document section and contains prudent hedging rather than suspicious absolutes. Without access to the source, no self-evident misrepresentation is detectable.
